statistics_probability.question: Why are statistics and probability important in Machine Learning?
What is the difference between population and sample?
What are mean, median, and mode?
What is variance?
What is standard deviation?
What is probability?
What is a probability distribution?
What is the difference between discrete and continuous random variables?
What is correlation?
Why does correlation not necessarily imply causation?
Explain the bias-variance trade-off from a statistical perspective.
What is the Central Limit Theorem, and why is it important in Machine Learning?
Explain confidence intervals and their practical significance.
What is hypothesis testing, and when would you use it?
How would you identify outliers in a dataset using statistical methods?
Explain the difference between covariance and correlation.
Why are Gaussian distributions commonly assumed in Machine Learning?
How would you determine whether a dataset follows a normal distribution?
What statistical problems can arise from using a very small dataset?
How would you determine whether two variables have a statistically significant relationship?
How would you determine whether poor model performance is caused by noisy data or insufficient data?
Explain the statistical assumptions behind linear regression and describe how violations affect model performance.
How would you evaluate whether collecting more data is likely to improve model performance?
How would you design an experiment to compare two machine learning models while minimizing statistical bias?
Explain the relationship between probability distributions, likelihood, and Bayesian inference.
How would you determine whether feature distributions have changed significantly between training and production data?
Describe statistical techniques for detecting data drift in production machine learning systems.
How would you design a statistically sound A/B test for evaluating a new machine learning model?
Explain the trade-offs between statistical significance and practical significance when evaluating model improvements.
How would you evaluate uncertainty in predictions generated by a machine learning model?
A stakeholder claims that a new model is better because its accuracy increased by 1%. Explain how you would determine whether the improvement is statistically meaningful.
A production model suddenly begins making inaccurate predictions after six months. Describe the statistical analyses you would perform before retraining the model.
A teammate proposes removing every detected outlier before training a model. How would you evaluate whether this is an appropriate decision?
Two machine learning models achieve nearly identical accuracy scores. Walk me through the statistical methods you would use to determine which model should be deployed.
A customer provides a dataset containing only a few hundred samples. Explain how you would assess whether it is sufficient for training a reliable machine learning model.
Your monitoring system reports that the distribution of several production features has changed significantly. Describe how you would investigate the issue.
A product manager requests deployment of a model that performs well on average but poorly for a small subset of users. Explain how you would evaluate this decision.
A new feature appears highly correlated with the target variable. Describe how you would determine whether it represents meaningful information or data leakage.
After retraining, model performance improves on the validation set but declines in production. Walk me through the statistical evidence you would gather to explain the discrepancy.

You're responsible for validating a machine learning model before deployment in a healthcare application. Explain the statistical analyses, validation strategy, uncertainty estimation, and monitoring plan you would implement.
